title: Freight Rail Car Utilization Collapse Signals Logistics Downturn
thesis: Railroad freight car utilization drops sharply when industrial demand weakens, signaling recession pressure on logistics and manufacturing. Weekly AAR reports are consistent and predictable. Low freight utilization precedes industrial slowdown and manufacturing contraction, directly impacting industrial equipment and transportation stocks.
data_sources: Association of American Railroads weekly freight car availability and utilization through html_table adapter
signal_logic: If weekly freight car utilization falls more than 8% below its 12-week moving average, enter short.
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly freight car utilization falls more than 8% below its 12-week moving average, enter short. Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or when utilization rebounds to within 3% of 12-week MA.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Association of American Railroads weekly freight car availability and utilization through html_table adapter via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Freight utilization swings 8%+ several times per quarter in normal economic cycles, making it a frequent signal generator.

schema.idea: Freight Rail Car Utilization Collapse Signals Logistics Downturn

data.source: Association of American Railroads weekly freight car availability and utilization through html_table adapter

schema.behavior: Railroad freight car utilization drops sharply when industrial demand weakens, signaling recession pressure on logistics and manufacturing. Weekly AAR reports are consistent and predictable.

market_impact.rationale: Low freight utilization precedes industrial slowdown and manufacturing contraction, directly impacting industrial equipment and transportation stocks.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly freight car utilization falls more than 8% below its 12-week moving average, enter short.
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or when utilization rebounds to within 3% of 12-week MA.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Freight utilization swings 8%+ several times per quarter in normal economic cycles, making it a frequent signal generator.

title: Commercial Real Estate Vacancy Spike Bearish Signal
thesis: Weekly CRE vacancy announcements and market reports often spike when major tenant departures or defaults occur, signaling real estate distress and reducing property valuations. Rising CRE vacancy directly compresses real estate fundamentals, cap rates, and investor returns in the real estate sector.
data_sources: CoStar or CBRE weekly commercial vacancy rate data through html_table adapter
signal_logic: If reported CRE vacancy rate rises 0.5% or more week-over-week in any major metro, short XLRE.
entry_exit: Entry: If reported CRE vacancy rate rises 0.5% or more week-over-week in any major metro, short XLRE. Exit: Exit after 12 trading days or when weekly vacancy stabilizes.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use CoStar or CBRE weekly commercial vacancy rate data through html_table adapter via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: CRE vacancies fluctuate weekly and 0.5%+ moves occur multiple times per quarter as tenants relocate or default.

schema.idea: Commercial Real Estate Vacancy Spike Bearish Signal

data.source: CoStar or CBRE weekly commercial vacancy rate data through html_table adapter

schema.behavior: Weekly CRE vacancy announcements and market reports often spike when major tenant departures or defaults occur, signaling real estate distress and reducing property valuations.

market_impact.rationale: Rising CRE vacancy directly compresses real estate fundamentals, cap rates, and investor returns in the real estate sector.

trade_logic.entry: If reported CRE vacancy rate rises 0.5% or more week-over-week in any major metro, short XLRE.
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 12 trading days or when weekly vacancy stabilizes.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: CRE vacancies fluctuate weekly and 0.5%+ moves occur multiple times per quarter as tenants relocate or default.

title: College Application Volume Collapse Signals Education Demand Crisis
thesis: Dramatic drops in college application volumes during peak application season signal demographic or affordability collapse, reducing future revenue for education services and related consumer spending. Lower application volumes signal weakening middle-class household formation, future discretionary spending, and consumer confidence in education ROI.
data_sources: Common App weekly application submission totals through html_table adapter from public reports
signal_logic: If weekly Common App submissions fall 15% or more below the prior-year same week, short XLY.
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Common App submissions fall 15% or more below the prior-year same week, short XLY. Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or when submissions recover to prior-year levels.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Common App weekly application submission totals through html_table adapter from public reports via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Common App data is released weekly during application season (Sept–Jan) and 15%+ year-over-year swings happen several times per cycle.

data.source: Common App weekly application submission totals through html_table adapter from public reports

market_impact.rationale: Lower application volumes signal weakening middle-class household formation, future discretionary spending, and consumer confidence in education ROI.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Common App submissions fall 15% or more below the prior-year same week, short XLY.
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or when submissions recover to prior-year levels.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Common App data is released weekly during application season (Sept–Jan) and 15%+ year-over-year swings happen several times per cycle.

title: Trucking Load Board Capacity Surge Signals Freight Oversupply
thesis: When available loads vastly outnumber available trucks on public load boards, trucking capacity crashes and freight rates collapse, signaling logistics sector weakness. Trucking oversupply indicates weak shipping demand, depresses carrier margins, and signals industrial slowdown.
data_sources: Truckstop.com and Uber Freight public load board fill-rate indices through html_table or api adapter
signal_logic: If daily load board available-loads-to-trucks ratio rises above 2.0 for 3 consecutive days, short XLI.
entry_exit: Entry: If daily load board available-loads-to-trucks ratio rises above 2.0 for 3 consecutive days, short XLI. Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or when ratio falls below 1.5.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Truckstop.com and Uber Freight public load board fill-rate indices through html_table or api adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Load board ratios spike and crash weekly based on seasonal and cyclical freight demand, firing multiple times per month.

schema.idea: Trucking Load Board Capacity Surge Signals Freight Oversupply

data.source: Truckstop.com and Uber Freight public load board fill-rate indices through html_table or api adapter

schema.behavior: When available loads vastly outnumber available trucks on public load boards, trucking capacity crashes and freight rates collapse, signaling logistics sector weakness.

market_impact.rationale: Trucking oversupply indicates weak shipping demand, depresses carrier margins, and signals industrial slowdown.

trade_logic.entry: If daily load board available-loads-to-trucks ratio rises above 2.0 for 3 consecutive days, short XLI.
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or when ratio falls below 1.5.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Load board ratios spike and crash weekly based on seasonal and cyclical freight demand, firing multiple times per month.

title: Temporary Staffing Agency Hiring Collapse Signals Labor Market Deterioration
thesis: Weekly announcements of collapsed temporary hiring intentions from major staffing agencies precede broader labor market deterioration and consumer spending weakness. Temp hiring collapse signals companies expect weak demand, reducing consumer spending power and leading to discretionary spending cuts.
data_sources: ManpowerGroup and Kforce weekly hiring intention indices from earnings call transcripts and press releases through rss_count adapter
signal_logic: If weekly temp hiring intention index falls 12% or more below 3-month MA, short XLY.
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly temp hiring intention index falls 12% or more below 3-month MA, short XLY. Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or when index stabilizes above MA.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use ManpowerGroup and Kforce weekly hiring intention indices from earnings call transcripts and press releases through rss_count adapter via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Temp hiring swings 12%+ quarterly and reports are published weekly, creating 3–5 signals per quarter.

market_impact.rationale: Temp hiring collapse signals companies expect weak demand, reducing consumer spending power and leading to discretionary spending cuts.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly temp hiring intention index falls 12% or more below 3-month MA, short XLY.
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or when index stabilizes above MA.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Temp hiring swings 12%+ quarterly and reports are published weekly, creating 3–5 signals per quarter.

title: Mortgage Application Volume Waterfall Signals Housing Demand Collapse
thesis: Sharp week-over-week drops in mortgage applications signal housing demand evaporates due to rate shocks or affordability crisis, impacting construction, materials, and consumer sentiment. Mortgage application collapses reduce demand for construction materials, lumber, and finished housing inventory.
data_sources: Mortgage Bankers Association weekly applications index through html_table adapter
signal_logic: If weekly mortgage applications fall 20% or more below prior week, short XLB for 10 days.
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly mortgage applications fall 20% or more below prior week, short XLB for 10 days. Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or when applications stabilize.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Mortgage Bankers Association weekly applications index through html_table adapter via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Mortgage applications swing 20%+ weekly due to rate announcements and seasonal factors, firing 2–4 times per month.

trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or when applications stabilize.

title: Natural Gas Storage Injection Collapse Signals Energy Demand Shock
thesis: When weekly natural gas storage injections plummet (or turn to unexpected draws), it signals either demand spike or supply shock, creating energy price volatility and sector stress. Unexpected storage draws or injection collapses support energy prices and create near-term energy sector gains from price recovery.
data_sources: EIA weekly natural gas storage updates through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If weekly EIA gas storage injection falls below historical 5-year minimum for that week by 50% or more, go long XLE.
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly EIA gas storage injection falls below historical 5-year minimum for that week by 50% or more, go long XLE. Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or when injection rebounds to normal levels.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use EIA weekly natural gas storage updates through fred_series adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Gas storage swings 50%+ vs. seasonal norms occur 4–8 times per year, particularly in winter and summer transition periods.

schema.idea: Natural Gas Storage Injection Collapse Signals Energy Demand Shock

data.source: EIA weekly natural gas storage updates through fred_series adapter

schema.behavior: When weekly natural gas storage injections plummet (or turn to unexpected draws), it signals either demand spike or supply shock, creating energy price volatility and sector stress.

market_impact.rationale: Unexpected storage draws or injection collapses support energy prices and create near-term energy sector gains from price recovery.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly EIA gas storage injection falls below historical 5-year minimum for that week by 50% or more, go long XLE.
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or when injection rebounds to normal levels.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Gas storage swings 50%+ vs. seasonal norms occur 4–8 times per year, particularly in winter and summer transition periods.

title: Credit Card Processing Volume Plunge Signals Consumer Spending Halt
thesis: Sharp drops in credit card transaction volumes precede broader consumer spending collapses and signal immediate consumer confidence crisis. Spending volume collapse directly compresses revenue for retail, hospitality, and consumer discretionary companies.
data_sources: Visa and Mastercard weekly transaction volume indices through html_table or rss_count adapter from earnings/investor updates
signal_logic: If weekly credit card transaction volume falls 15% or more below 4-week MA, short XLY.
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly credit card transaction volume falls 15% or more below 4-week MA, short XLY. Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or when volume recovers to MA.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Visa and Mastercard weekly transaction volume indices through html_table or rss_count adapter from earnings/investor updates via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Credit card volumes experience 15%+ swings quarterly due to seasonal patterns and economic shocks, creating 2–4 monthly signals.

title: Copper Futures Volatility Spike Signals Risk-off Macro Repricing
thesis: Copper volatility spikes often precede broad industrial and emerging market stress, as copper is a leading macroeconomic risk proxy due to its use in construction and manufacturing. High copper volatility signals macro uncertainty, reducing industrial capex and equipment demand.
data_sources: Copper futures daily prices and 20-day volatility through price_only adapter
signal_logic: If 20-day copper futures volatility rises 40% or more above its 60-day MA, short XLI.
entry_exit: Entry: If 20-day copper futures volatility rises 40% or more above its 60-day MA, short XLI. Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or when copper volatility mean-reverts below MA + 20%.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Copper futures daily prices and 20-day volatility through price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Copper volatility swings 40%+ on macro headlines and Fed announcements 2–4 times per month.

schema.idea: Copper Futures Volatility Spike Signals Risk-Off Macro Repricing

data.source: Copper futures daily prices and 20-day volatility through price_only adapter

schema.behavior: Copper volatility spikes often precede broad industrial and emerging market stress, as copper is a leading macroeconomic risk proxy due to its use in construction and manufacturing.

market_impact.rationale: High copper volatility signals macro uncertainty, reducing industrial capex and equipment demand.

trade_logic.entry: If 20-day copper futures volatility rises 40% or more above its 60-day MA, short XLI.
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or when copper volatility mean-reverts below MA + 20%.

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Copper volatility swings 40%+ on macro headlines and Fed announcements 2–4 times per month.

title: Regional Bank Cds Spread Widening Signals Financial System Stress
thesis: When regional bank credit default swap spreads widen sharply, it signals market-perceived stress in the mid-sized financial system, often preceding credit market seizures or deposit runs. CDS spread widening indicates markets price higher default risk for regional banks, compressing equity valuations and reducing lending.
data_sources: FRED regional bank CDS indices (5-year spreads) and daily price tracking through fred_series adapter
signal_logic: If daily regional bank CDS spread widens 30+ basis points in a single day or rises 50+ bps over 5 days, short XLF.
entry_exit: Entry: If daily regional bank CDS spread widens 30+ basis points in a single day or rises 50+ bps over 5 days, short XLF. Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or when spreads contract 20+ bps.
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED regional bank CDS indices (5-year spreads) and daily price tracking through fred_series adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Regional bank CDS spreads experience 30+ bps daily moves during periods of financial uncertainty, which occur multiple times per quarter.

schema.idea: Regional Bank CDS Spread Widening Signals Financial System Stress

data.source: FRED regional bank CDS indices (5-year spreads) and daily price tracking through fred_series adapter

market_impact.rationale: CDS spread widening indicates markets price higher default risk for regional banks, compressing equity valuations and reducing lending.

trade_logic.entry: If daily regional bank CDS spread widens 30+ basis points in a single day or rises 50+ bps over 5 days, short XLF.
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or when spreads contract 20+ bps.
